news.headline: APA Corporation is offering to exchange or purchase up to $1 billion worth of Apache Corporation notes, with offers described in detail online. Eligible holders can participate in the offers.
news.summary: APA Corporation has announced the commencement of offers to exchange any and all tendered and accepted notes issued by its subsidiary, Apache Corporation, for new notes to be issued by APA. They have also initiated offers to purchase up to $1 billion aggregate principal amount of Apache notes for cash. Only eligible holders, which include qualified institutional buyers and non-U.S. persons, can participate in the offers. The offers are subject to applicable Series Cap and Maximum Purchase Amount. The eligibility letter for participation is available on www.dfking.com/apache.

news.headline: VasCare in Corpus Christi, Texas, adds vascular surgeon Dr. Jenny Mike-Mayer, expands facility, and upgrades website.
news.summary: A healthcare company in Corpus Christi, Texas, VasCare, has recently welcomed vascular surgeon Dr. Jenny Mike-Mayer to their team. Along with this addition, the company has expanded its facility and launched a new website.

news.headline: Anchorage Int'l Film Festival closing block features "Drum Song: The Rhythm of Life" in Native Voices showcase at Bear Tooth Theatrepub on Dec 3, 2024, at 4pm.
news.summary: On December 3, 2024, in Anchorage, Alaska, a film titled DRUM SONG: THE RHYTHM OF LIFE is set to be showcased during the concluding segment of the Anchorage International Film Festival. This event is part of the NATIVE VOICES exhibit and will take place at the Bear Tooth Theatrepub, situated at 1230 W 27th Ave in Anchorage. The screening is scheduled for 4:00 pm.

news.headline: J. M. Smucker Co. offers cash tender for max principal amount of validly tendered debt - Dec. 3, 2024.
news.summary: Orville, Ohio - On December 3, 2024, The J.M. Smucker Company initiated cash tender offers (referred to as "Offers") for the highest principal sum of eligible tenders that have been submitted (and not withdrawn) within the company (NYSE: SJM).

news.headline: Lockheed Martin appoints Kevin O'Connor as new general counsel and corporate secretary, succeeding Maryanne Lavan. Effective Jan. 13.
news.summary: In Bethesda, Maryland on December 3, 2024, the Board of Directors at Lockheed Martin (NYSE: LMT) announced the appointment of Kevin O'Connor as the new senior vice president, general counsel, and corporate secretary. Taking over from Maryanne Lavan, O'Connor will assume the role on January 13 following Lavan's retirement.

news.headline: TXNM Energy raises annual dividend by $0.08 (5.2% increase) announced on Dec. 3, 2024.
news.summary: TXNM Energy and Public Service Company of New Mexico announced their quarterly dividends on Dec. 3, 2024. The Board of Directors of TXNM Energy (NYSE: TXNM) approved a 5.2% increase in the annual dividend payment, raising it by $0.08. The company's dividend is now set at an indicated amount...

news.headline: Rubicon secures $20M in equity raise and refinances debt facilities for waste solutions.
news.summary: Atlanta-based Rubicon Technologies, Inc., a prominent provider of waste and recycling solutions, unveiled a refinancing move involving certain existing debt facilities. This includes the introduction of a new revolving facility and the raising of $20 million through preferred equity. The transactions received approval from an independent special committee and the company's Board of Directors. Rodina Capital, a private investment firm, is involved in the deal. Rubicon's OTC symbol is RBTC.

news.headline: Broadridge wins Nicsa NOVA "Innovation in Operations" award for its Tailored Shareholder Reports solution in 2024.
news.summary: On December 3, 2024, in New York, Nicsa, a leading asset and wealth management industry association, honored Broadridge Financial Solutions, Inc. (NYSE:BR) with the 2024 NOVA "Innovation in Operations" award. This recognition was awarded for their Tailored Shareholder Reports solution, crafted to tackle specific issues within the industry.

news.headline: Hess Midstream LP will partake in Wells Fargo Annual Midstream, Energy & Utilities Symposium in Houston.
news.summary: Houston-based Hess Midstream LP has announced its participation in the upcoming Wells Fargo Annual Midstream, Energy, and Utilities Symposium. The event will provide an opportunity for the company to discuss its operations and future prospects. Hess Midstream LP will join other industry leaders in addressing key stakeholders, investors, and analysts. This participation emphasizes the company's commitment to transparency and engagement within the sector.

news.headline: AEC praises CBP for finding forced labor by Kingtom Aluminio S.R.L. in Dominican Republic in violation of Tariff Act.
news.summary: In Wauconda, Illinois, the Aluminum Extruders Council (AEC) has praised U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) for taking action by issuing a forced labor finding against Kingtom Aluminio S.R.L. CBP found that aluminum extrusions and profile products made by Kingtom in the Dominican Republic were produced using convict, forced, or indentured labor, violating Section 307 of the Tariff Act of 1930. This decision followed a detailed investigation conducted by CBP.

news.headline: Americold's Russellville facility wins 2024 Built By The Best Award for design, construction, and operations excellence.
news.summary: Americold Realty Trust (NYSE: COLD) received the prestigious 2024 Built By The Best Award from the Controlled Environment Building Association (CEBA) for its Russellville, Arkansas facility. This recognition showcases Americold's leading design, construction innovation, and operational excellence. The facility was also acknowledged as Site of the Year by one of Americold's major clients due to its seamless start-up and successful ramp-up to full capacity. This further solidifies Americold's reputation for outstanding performance in the industry.

news.headline: FTAI Aviation sells two offshore energy vessels for $143 million, shifts focus to aftermarket engine power.
news.summary: In a recent announcement in New York on December 3, 2024, FTAI Aviation Ltd. (Nasdaq: FTAI) reported the successful sale of its last offshore energy vessels - the Pioneer and the Pride, achieving a total transaction value of around $143 million. The sale aligns with FTAI's strategic decision to concentrate on its primary operations concerning power provision for the aftermarket segments of the leading commercial engine programs, namely the CFM56 and the V2500. This move signifies FTAI's commitment to enhancing and reinforcing its position in these core business areas.

news.headline: Alliance Entertainment Holding Corporation (Nasdaq: AENT) to host investor webinar on Dec 10 at 4:15 p.m. ET.
news.summary: A global distributor and wholesaler, Alliance Entertainment Holding Corporation (Nasdaq: AENT), with headquarters in Plantation, Fla., has announced a webinar scheduled for December 10, 2024, at 4:15 p.m. Eastern Time. The company, specializing in various products including music, movies, video games, electronics, arcades, toys, and collectibles, invites investors to attend this virtual event.

news.headline: Enact Mortgage Insurance signs two reinsurance agreements, securing 27% coverage from third-party providers.
news.summary: Acquires 27% of reinsurance coverage for its forward quota share from a consortium of third-party reinsurance providers.

news.headline: Mural Oncology grants stock options and restricted stock units to 4 new employees, totaling 26,300 and 13,200 shares respectively.
news.summary: On December 3, 2024, Mural Oncology plc, a clinical-stage immuno-oncology company traded on Nasdaq as MURA, based in Waltham, Mass and Dublin, announced that it granted four new employees non-statutory stock options to buy a total of 26,300 ordinary shares and restricted stock units for 13,200 ordinary shares. These inducements were part of the Company’s 2024 Inducement Stock Option and Incentive Plan to attract and retain talent, as per Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).

news.headline: Onity Group sells 15% interest in MSR Asset Vehicle LLC to Oaktree for $49.5 million. Deal finalized.
news.summary: In West Palm Beach, Florida, Onity Group Inc. (NYSE: ONIT) revealed the finalization of its deal with Oaktree Capital Management, L.P. to sell its 15% stake in MSR Asset Vehicle LLC (MAV) for $49.5 million in cash. Onity, a key player in non-bank mortgage servicing and originations, closed the transaction with Oaktree as announced, marking a significant financial move.

news.headline: Uniti Group Inc. execs to attend Raymond James 2024 conference in NY, December 9-10, 2024. Chief Revenue Officer and VP to participate.
news.summary: Little Rock, Arkansas - On December 3, 2024, Uniti Group Inc. revealed that its Chief Revenue Officer, Ron Mudry, and Vice President of Investor Relations & Treasury, Bill DiTullio, will attend the Raymond James 2024 TMT & Consumer Conference in New York, NY, on December 9-10, 2024. Uniti is listed on Nasdaq as UNIT.

news.headline: Couchbase (NASDAQ: BASE) Q3 earnings report out - beat estimates by 38.0% with EPS of $-0.05. More on Benzinga.com.
news.summary: Couchbase (NASDAQ:BASE) recently published its Q3 earnings report on Tuesday, December 3, 2024, at 04:05 PM. The report revealed significant news for investors. In terms of earnings, Couchbase surpassed projected earnings by an impressive 38.0%, disclosing an EPS of $-0.05. For a detailed analysis, refer to the complete report on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Federal Reserve expected to cut rates. Size of cut uncertain. Markets volatile post-Fed meetings.
news.summary: Rate cuts from the Federal Reserve are on the horizon, with Wednesday's potential move still uncertain. It is common for markets to experience volatility following a Fed meeting. Investors are advised to prepare for potential Fed action and market fluctuations.

news.headline: Retail sales report released before Fed meeting for potential rate cut. S&P 500 nears record high.
news.summary: The latest retail sales data serves as the final significant economic update prior to the upcoming Fed meeting, where the first rate-cut in years is anticipated. The S&P 500 is currently close to reaching a record high, reflecting positive market sentiment.

news.headline: Fed expected to cut rates by half a point as S&P 500 eyes new high amidst wild reactions. Wall Street anticipates new easing cycle.
news.summary: The upcoming Federal Reserve meeting this week is set to initiate a new phase of monetary easing. Experts predict a 0.5% reduction in interest rates, with the S&P 500 index poised to reach record levels. Following the Fed meeting, significant rate cuts are anticipated, sparking a surge in the S&P 500's performance. The reaction of the market index could be particularly volatile in response to these developments.

news.headline: Upcoming Fed meeting on Sept. 17-18 will discuss rate cuts and its impact on S&P 500. Investors eagerly await decisions.
news.summary: Investors always anticipate Federal Reserve meetings, with the one scheduled for September 17-18 being particularly significant. This meeting will focus on the Fed's rate-cut outlook and its potential impact on the S&P 500. Such meetings tend to have a noteworthy effect on the stock market and are closely monitored by investors.

news.headline: Fed cuts rates for the first time since March 2020, S&P 500 pauses; Powell optimistic about US economy.
news.summary: In a significant move, the Federal Reserve implemented its first rate cut since March 2020, causing a slight dip in the S&P 500. Chairman Jerome Powell expressed optimism regarding the US economy. This step received significant attention as it marked a notable change in policies. The decision coincided with a pause in the S&P 500's performance. This development signals a potential shift in economic strategies amidst ongoing market fluctuations.

news.headline: Cibus Inc. reorganizes with $10 million in projected cost savings annually, expecting 20% cash use reduction.
news.summary: Estimated to save around $10 million annually and cut cash usage by roughly 20%, resulting in significant cost reductions. This initiative is expected to yield a substantial financial impact.

news.headline: Positive market sentiment as Trump's Treasury Secretary pick boosts investor confidence. Potential rate cut chances drop to 56%. Futures up: Nasdaq 0.58%, S&P 0.49%, Dow Jones 0.68%, R2K 1.12%. Last week saw Dow up 2%, S&P 1.7%, Nasdaq 1.6%. Services PMI at 57, manufacturing at 48.8. US consumer sentiment at 71.8. Biggest gains in industrials, financials, and consumer discretionary sectors. Wells Fargo analysts optimistic on food inflation cooling.
news.summary: U.S. stocks are likely to open positively on Monday following the Dow Jones closing at a new record high. Treasury Secretary pick Scott Bessent's plans to make Trump's tax cuts permanent boosted investor sentiment. Expectations of a December rate cut have lowered to 56%. Futures show gains: Nasdaq 0.58%, S&P 500 0.49%, Dow Jones 0.68%, R2K 1.12%. Pre-market trading shows SPDR S&P 500 ETF up 0.54% at $598.72 and Invesco QQQ ETF up 0.64% at $509.01. Last week, major indices saw gains with Dow up 2%, S&P 500 up 1.7%, and Nasdaq up 1.6%. Economic data showed an increase in the S&P Global US Services PMI to 57 and manufacturing PMI to 48.8, but the University of Michigan consumer sentiment fell to 71.8. S&P 500 sectors like industrials saw gains while utilities and communication services stocks dropped. Index performance for the week: Nasdaq +1.64%, S&P 500 +1.7%, Dow Jones +2.18%, Russell 2000 +4.54%. Wells Fargo analysts are optimistic about food inflation cooling down.

news.headline: Gran Tierra Energy Inc. announces pricing of $150M additional principal amount of its 9.500% Senior Secured Notes due 2029.
news.summary: Today in Calgary, Alberta, Gran Tierra Energy Inc. announced the pricing of an additional $150 million aggregate principal amount of its previously issued 9.500% Senior Secured Amortizing Notes due 2029 in a private placement. The Notes were offered to qualified institutional buyers in the US under Rule 144A of the Securities Act and to non-US persons outside the US under Regulation S of the Securities Act, as well as through certain prospectus exemptions in Canada. Gran Tierra is listed on the NYSE American, TSX, and LSE under the ticker symbol GTE.

news.headline: Economist Steve Hanke dismisses concerns of inflation under Trump, predicts Fed will keep prices below 2% by 2025.
news.summary: Economist Steve Hanke addressed concerns about potential inflation under the new administration, dismissing fears of a significant price surge. In an interview with CNBC, Hanke emphasized that the inflation trajectory hinges more on Federal Reserve actions rather than proposed economic policies. He stated that the talk of Trump's policies leading to inflation is unfounded. Hanke pointed out the U.S. money supply as a critical factor, noting its contraction since 2022, historically preceding economic downturns. Such contractions have raised alarms about potential economic challenges.

news.headline: Fed's rate cut possibility tied to core PCE inflation trends. S&P 500 up. Job growth rates slowing.
news.summary: The possibility of more Federal Reserve rate cuts depends on the trend of core PCE inflation, with a focus on moderation. Additionally, a decrease in job growth could also lead to further rate reductions. The recent data indicates that core PCE inflation remains under control, keeping the option for another significant Fed rate cut viable. Following this news, the S&P 500 index saw an increase in value.

news.headline: Upwork plans organizational changes for growth, with Q3 2024 results surpassing financial expectations.
news.summary: A record number of new COVID-19 cases were reported in the past 24 hours, with over 20,000 new infections confirmed. This surge brings the total number of cases in the country to over 500,000. Health officials are urging the public to follow safety guidelines and practice social distancing to curb the spread of the virus. Hospitalizations have also increased significantly, with over 2,000 new patients admitted in the last week. The death toll has surpassed 10,000, as 150 new fatalities were reported today. Authorities are considering implementing stricter measures to control the worsening situation.

news.headline: Fed's Powell downplays rate cut odds, emphasizes healthy consumer. S&P 500 rises despite lessened chance of cut.
news.summary: Jerome Powell, Chairman of the Federal Reserve, reduced the likelihood of a significant rate cut and highlighted a stronger consumer market. Despite this, the S&P 500 index experienced an increase. This news suggests that the chances of a large rate reduction have decreased, indicating a more positive outlook on the economy.

news.headline: Analysis: Impact of Trump's mass deportations, tariffs, and Fed decisions on economy uncertain. Economists predict significant growth and inflation challenges.
news.summary: Economists are currently analyzing the potential impact of Trump’s policies on economic growth and inflation levels. The wide range of possible outcomes has led to significant uncertainties in their predictions.

news.headline: Prospera Energy Inc. reports 3 years of restructuring, recovery, and future growth, announced on Oct. 24, 2024.
news.summary: On October 24, 2024, in Calgary, Alberta, Prospera Energy Inc. ("PEI") made an announcement. PEI is listed on the TSX Ventures Exchange under the ticker symbol PEI, on the OTC market under GXRFF, and on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange under OF6B.

news.headline: Local bank set to become region's largest with Foresight Financial Group merger, solidifying their presence.
Global Jet Capital completes BJETS 2024-2 securitization, raising $617M, total securitized assets now $5.8B, bonds $4.8B.
news.summary: In Danbury, CT on September 17, 2024, Global Jet Capital, a prominent player in providing financial services for business aircraft, declared the completion of its BJETS 2024-2 securitization, gathering around $617 million. The BJETS 2024-2 marks the eighth asset-backed security (ABS) offering by Global Jet Capital, resulting in total securitized assets of about $5.8 billion and bonds issued totaling around $4.8 billion.

news.headline: ADP Jobs report shows hiring increase, lowering chances of significant Fed rate cut; S&P 500 futures decline.
news.summary: The latest ADP jobs report reflects a decrease in hiring similar to official data, resulting in a significant Fed rate cut last month. This contributed to the S&P 500 reaching new record levels. Following the release of the ADP Jobs Report, there has been an increase in hiring, reducing the likelihood of a substantial Fed rate cut. As a result, S&P 500 futures have slightly declined in response to this news.

news.headline: Hepsiburada announces second issuance of asset-backed securities totaling TRY 350 million on Sept. 30, 2024.
news.summary: Istanbul, September 30, 2024 - Today, the prominent Turkish e-commerce platform D-MARKET Electronic Services & Trading, also known as "Hepsiburada," revealed the launch of their second asset-backed securities issuance totaling TRY 350 million. The company, listed on NASDAQ as HEPS, is making significant strides in their financial endeavors.

news.headline: Trump's fortune faces uncertainty as his political future hangs on election; stakes high for his stock-market riches.
news.summary: The outcome of the upcoming presidential election this week will play a crucial role in determining the future of Donald Trump's political career. In addition, a significant portion of his wealth is also at stake.

news.headline: Azerion seeks EUR 50 million through Subsequent Bonds listing on Nasdaq Stockholm; prospectus published.
news.summary: This information should not be distributed to any individual in the United States of America or to any U.S. person (as defined in Regulation S under the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, as amended) or in any jurisdiction where it is illegal to distribute this document.

news.headline: Eterna Therapeutics completes financial restructuring to boost development and success.
news.summary: Eterna Therapeutics has successfully concluded a strategic financial restructuring initiative to enhance its developmental endeavors and pave the way for long-term prosperity. The restructuring seeks to expedite the company's activities and ensure sustained growth. This move involves significant investment and aims to bolster Eterna Therapeutics' position in the market.

news.headline: US stocks face a slow start after Trump's tariff threats vs Canada & Mexico. Sectors linked to AI remain optimistic. Indices in futures trading: Nasdaq 100 +0.08%, S&P 500 +0.09%, Dow Jones +0.07%, R2K -0.26%. Dow Jones closed at 44,736.57, S&P 500 at 5,987.37, Nasdaq Composite at 19,054.84. Dollar Index at 107 level. Chicago Fed Index fell to -0.40, while Texas manufacturing index rose to -2.7. Real estate, materials, and consumer discretionary sectors saw gains but energy & IT saw losses. Fed rate cut odds at 59%.
news.summary: US stocks are expected to open sluggishly following President-elect Donald Trump's threats of tariffs on Canada and Mexico. The Dollar Index is at 107, with rate cut expectations for December at 59% (down from 75% a month ago). Futures are mixed, with Nasdaq and S&P 500 up around 0.08-0.09%, Dow Jones up 0.07%, and Russell 2000 down 0.26%. In premarket trading, SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust rose 0.06% to $597.87 and Invesco QQQ ETF rose 0.04% to $506.80. On Monday, Dow Jones closed at 44,736.57, S&P 500 at 5,987.37, and Nasdaq Composite at 19,054.84. Economic data showed Chicago Fed National Activity Index fell to -0.40 and Dallas Fed’s Texas manufacturing activity index rose to -2.7. Most S&P sectors closed positively, with real estate, materials, and consumer discretionary stocks gaining, while energy and information technology stocks declined.

news.headline: Trump's team includes China hawks and business allies like Elon Musk, sparking speculation on his stance towards China.
news.summary: The incoming president, Donald J. Trump, is in the process of forming a team of advisors who hold a strong stance against China. It is noteworthy that among his advisors, there are individuals who engage in business dealings with China, most notably Elon Musk. The dynamic of the team suggests a mix of approximately X confrontational aides and Y business-oriented advisors when it comes to dealing with Chinese relations. This blend of perspectives could potentially shape the future direction of the U.S.-China relationship under the new administration.

news.headline: Investors prepare for political volatility with contested election outcome, anticipating changes in interest rates.
news.summary: In anticipation of a potentially disputed election result, investors are preparing for political unrest in the period leading up to the Inauguration Day.

news.headline: Pres. Trump criticized Fed for raising interest rates, saying it's impacting economic growth, with Dow down 6.7%, worst since 2008.
Logging, a dangerous job, is a way of life in rural Oregon and beyond with high fatalities in the industry.
news.summary: For many rural communities in the Pacific Northwest and the southern United States, logging has been a traditional means of livelihood. However, this practice is not without its dangers.

news.headline: NowCM and White & Case collaborated to create Haniel's pioneering fully digital commercial paper program.
Strong job report decreases chance of large Fed rate cut, S&P 500 up, near record high. Economy impacted.
news.summary: The recent jobs report released on Friday has significant implications for Federal Reserve rate cuts and the U.S. economy. The strong hiring numbers have reduced the likelihood of a substantial rate cut by the Federal Reserve. However, this development does not seem to have affected the S&P 500, which is currently close to reaching a record high. This surprising information indicates that while the job market is thriving, the impact on the stock market may not be as pronounced.

news.headline: 10-year Treasury yield surpasses 4% amid Fed rate cut doubts post strong jobs report; S&P 500 dips.
news.summary: The 10-year Treasury yield has exceeded 4% following a reassessment in the markets regarding the necessity of Federal Reserve rate cuts, in response to the robust September jobs report. The S&P 500 experienced a slight decline amidst these developments.

news.headline: Matinas BioPharma terminates MAT2203 partnership negotiations, resulting in an 80% workforce reduction eliminating 15 positions.
news.summary: In Bedminster, New Jersey, on October 31, 2024, Matinas BioPharma Holdings, Inc. (NYSE American: MTNB) revealed the cessation of negotiations concerning global rights to MAT2203. The termination, initiated by the prospective partner, led to a significant decision by Matinas. An immediate 80% reduction in the workforce was implemented, resulting in the elimination of 15 positions, including three senior management members. Additionally, all product development operations have been halted to preserve cash flow.

news.headline: Stock market mixed as Fed minutes awaited. Mexican peso hits 2-year low, falling 2.5% to 22.80 per U.S. dollar amid tariff fears.
news.summary: The US stock market showed mixed movements as investors awaited the release of the Federal Reserve's November meeting minutes. Fed futures predict a 59% chance of a 25-basis-point rate cut in December, with remaining odds pointing to steady rates in the 4.5%-4.75% range. President-elect Donald Trump's pledge to raise tariffs to 25% on all imports from Mexico and Canada led to a surge in volatility, with the Mexican peso dropping 2.5% to 22.80 per US dollar. The iShares MSCI Mexico ETF (NYSE:EWW) also saw effects from this announcement.

news.headline: Sylogist renews its Normal Course Issuer Bid program, allowing it to repurchase up to 1 million shares.
